examples.title: Security Group Rule Validator
examples.description: Python script to validate security group rules for common security issues
examples.explanation: This script iterates through all security groups in an AWS account and identifies potentially dangerous configurations like exposing SSH/RDP to the internet or allowing unrestricted outbound traffic.

examples.title: IAM Policy Generator with Least Privilege
examples.description: Generate IAM policies following least privilege principle for specific actions
examples.explanation: Demonstrates how to create IAM policies following the principle of least privilege by explicitly specifying actions and resources instead of using wildcards, and includes validation to identify overly permissive patterns.

examples.title: Input Validation Sanitizer
examples.description: Security middleware for input validation to prevent SQL injection and XSS attacks
examples.explanation: Provides reusable input validation functions to prevent common application-layer attacks including SQL injection patterns, XSS through HTML encoding, path traversal, and format validation. Note: For production, use parameterized queries for SQL and Content Security Policy for XSS.

examples.title: CloudFormation Security Group Template
examples.description: Infrastructure-as-Code template for secure VPC and security group setup
examples.code:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: 'Secure VPC Architecture with Security Groups'

Resources:
  # VPC Configuration
  MyVPC:
    Type: AWS::EC2::VPC
    Properties:
      CidrBlock: '10.0.0.0/16'
      EnableDnsHostnames: true
      EnableDnsSupport: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: SecureVPC

  # Public Subnet
  PublicSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      CidrBlock: '10.0.1.0/24'
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      MapPublicIpOnLaunch: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PublicSubnet

  # Private Subnet (Application Tier)
  PrivateSubnet1:
    Type: AWS::EC2::Subnet
    Properties:
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      CidrBlock: '10.0.10.0/24'
      AvailabilityZone: !Select [0, !GetAZs '']
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: PrivateSubnet

  # Security Group for Application Load Balancer
  ALBSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: 'Security group for ALB - HTTPS only'
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: '0.0.0.0/0'
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 80
          ToPort: 80
          CidrIp: '0.0.0.0/0'
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: ALB-SecurityGroup

  # Security Group for Application Servers
  AppSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: 'Security group for App servers'
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 443
          ToPort: 443
          SourceSecurityGroupId: !Ref ALBSecurityGroup
      SecurityGroupEgress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          ToPort: 443
          CidrIp: '10.0.20.0/24'  # To RDS
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: AppServer-SecurityGroup

  # Security Group for RDS Database
  RDSSecurityGroup:
    Type: AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: 'Security group for RDS - DB access only from App'
      VpcId: !Ref MyVPC
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: 5432
          ToPort: 5432
          SourceSecurityGroupId: !Ref AppSecurityGroup
      Tags:
        - Key: Name
          Value: RDS-SecurityGroup

Outputs:
  VPCId:
    Description: 'VPC ID'
    Value: !Ref MyVPC
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-VPC-ID'
examples.explanation: This CloudFormation template creates a secure three-tier VPC architecture with proper security group isolation: ALB accepts HTTPS traffic from the internet, App servers accept traffic only from the ALB, and RDS accepts connections only from the app security group. This implements defense in depth with least privilege network access.
